senses.definition: A traditional metal water jug or ewer from Persia and South Asia, with a handle and long spout, used for washing or pouring water.
examples: The antique aftaba on the shelf was made of engraved brass.
In some homes, guests wash their hands using water poured from an aftaba.
The museum displayed a 17th‑century Persian aftaba beside other copper vessels.

senses.definition: Later than a particular time or event; at a time that follows something else.
examples: We went for coffee after the meeting.
Please call me after six o’clock.
She felt much better after the operation.

senses.definition: Because of something that happened; as a result of something.
examples: After what happened, he decided to move to another city.
She never trusted him again after his lies.
The company changed its policy after the lawsuit.

senses.definition: In order or aiming to get or catch someone or something.
examples: The police are after the suspect.
He is after a new job in marketing.
The dog ran after the ball.
collocations: look after (different meaning but similar form)

senses.definition: Having the same name as someone, especially given in their honor.
examples: She was named after her grandmother.
The street is called after a famous writer.
Their son is named after his father.

senses.definition: Later; at a time that follows an earlier time or event.
examples: We went to the cinema and ate dinner after.
I will finish my work now and rest after.
At first it was hard, but it got easier after.

senses.definition: At a time later than when something else happened.
examples: After we finished dinner, we went for a walk.
He called me after he arrived.
After she had left, the room felt very quiet.

senses.definition: Towards or at the back part of a ship or aircraft.
examples: They stood on the after deck of the ship.
The after cabin was smaller than the one at the front.
The after section of the plane was reserved for staff.

senses.definition: Following someone or something in order or position.
examples: My name comes after yours on the list.
He walked in after me.
Letter B comes after A in the alphabet.

senses.definition: An act, event, or effect that follows another action, especially as a consequence or secondary result.
examples: The scandal was only the first problem; the political afteract was even more serious.
The social afteract of the decision lasted for many years.
Researchers studied the psychological afteract of the disaster.

senses.definition: Later life or old age; the period of life after the main or active years.
examples: He spent his afterage quietly in the countryside.
In her afterage, she wrote poetry and memoirs.
They planned carefully for comfort in their afterage.

senses.definition: A second or follow‑up attack that comes after an initial attack.
examples: Troops prepared for an expected afterattack.
The cyber‑security team strengthened defenses to prevent any afterattack.
The afterattack caused more damage than the first strike.

senses.definition: A strap or band at the back of something, especially on a horse’s harness or similar equipment.
examples: The afterband on the harness needed repair.
They adjusted the afterband to fit the horse correctly.
The leather afterband had started to crack with age.

senses.definition: A water channel or reservoir that receives water after it has passed through a dam, turbine, or lock.
examples: The discharged water flowed into the afterbay below the dam.
Engineers inspected the afterbay for signs of erosion.
Fish were seen gathering in the calm afterbay area.

senses.definition: A rhythmic beat that follows the main beat, often used to give music a certain drive or emphasis.
examples: The guitar stressed the afterbeat to create a strong groove.
Clapping on the afterbeat gave the song an energetic feel.
The drummer played a sharp afterbeat on the snare.

senses.definition: The tissue that comes out of the mother’s body after a baby is born, including the placenta and membranes.
examples: The midwife checked the afterbirth to make sure it was complete.
In some cultures, the afterbirth is buried in a special place.
The doctor removed the afterbirth shortly after delivery.

senses.definition: Plural of afterbirth: the tissues that come out of the mother’s body after babies are born.
examples: The vet examined the afterbirths of the newborn calves.
In the lab, scientists studied several afterbirths under the microscope.
The disposal of afterbirths must follow medical guidelines.

senses.definition: A later effect, result, or impression that comes after an event, especially an unpleasant one.
examples: The emotional afterblow of the argument stayed with her for days.
They were still feeling the financial afterblow of the crisis.
The scandal’s afterblow damaged the company’s image.

senses.definition: Plural of afterbody: the rear parts or sections of vehicles, especially ships or aircraft.
examples: The engineer inspected the afterbodies for structural damage.
Ice had formed on the afterbodies of the aircraft.
The shipyard focused on strengthening the afterbodies of older vessels.

senses.definition: The back part of a vehicle, especially a ship, aircraft, or car body.
examples: Designers changed the shape of the afterbody to reduce drag.
The afterbody of the ship was repaired in dry dock.
Damage to the afterbody affected the vessel’s balance.

senses.definition: The part of the brain at the back that includes the medulla, pons, and cerebellum; the hindbrain.
examples: The afterbrain controls many basic body functions.
Damage to the afterbrain can affect balance and breathing.
Researchers studied how the afterbrain develops in infants.

senses.definition: Plural of afterbrain: hindbrain regions in more than one brain or specimen.
examples: The study compared the afterbrains of several animal species.
They examined the afterbrains for signs of disease.
The afterbrains showed similar patterns of development.

senses.definition: A later break or opening that appears after an initial breach, especially in a wall, bank, or defense.
examples: Engineers quickly repaired the afterbreach in the flood bank.
The enemy took advantage of an afterbreach in the city wall.
The afterbreach caused more flooding than the first crack.

senses.definition: The back part of a breastplate or the rear part of a similar protective surface or structure.
examples: The armor included an afterbreast to protect the soldier’s back.
The wooden afterbreast on the cart needed replacement.
Adjustments were made to the afterbreast for a better fit.

senses.definition: A device in a jet engine that burns extra fuel in the exhaust to produce more power and speed.
examples: The fighter jet switched on its afterburner and climbed rapidly.
Using the afterburner increases fuel use but gives a strong thrust boost.
The pilot engaged the afterburner during takeoff.

senses.definition: Plural of afterburner: devices in jet engines that burn extra fuel in the exhaust to increase power and speed.
examples: Both engines’ afterburners were lit as the aircraft took off.
The noise from the afterburners was extremely loud.
Technicians inspected the afterburners for signs of wear.

senses.definition: The process of burning extra fuel in the exhaust of a jet engine to increase thrust.
examples: Afterburning allows the aircraft to reach supersonic speeds.
Continuous afterburning quickly uses up fuel.
The report explained the disadvantages of afterburning at low altitudes.

senses.definition: Relating to an afterburner or the use of extra fuel burning for more thrust.
examples: The aircraft has an afterburning turbojet engine.
Afterburning engines are common in fighter jets.
They compared afterburning and non‑afterburning versions of the same engine.

senses.definition: Multiple instances or periods of burning extra fuel in jet engine exhausts for additional thrust.
examples: The test included several afterburnings during the flight.
Repeated afterburnings placed stress on the engine components.
Engineers recorded the temperature changes during afterburnings.

senses.definition: Care or help given to someone after an operation, illness, or difficult experience.
examples: Good aftercare is essential after major surgery.
The clinic offers free aftercare for all patients.
Proper aftercare helps people recover faster.

senses.definition: Supervision or organized activities for children after normal school hours.
examples: Their daughter goes to aftercare until six o’clock.
The school runs an aftercare program for working parents.
Fees for aftercare are charged by the hour.

senses.definition: The work or life someone has after finishing their main career.
examples: She built a successful aftercareer as a consultant.
His aftercareer focused on charity work and mentoring.
Athletes often struggle to plan their aftercareer.

senses.definition: Plural of aftercare: different kinds of care or support provided after treatment or an event.
examples: The charity coordinates various aftercares for patients and families.
Different aftercares are needed for physical and emotional recovery.
They discussed the aftercares required following the procedure.

senses.definition: A later result or outcome that appears after an event, often used in technical or specialized contexts.
examples: Scientists monitored the aftercast of the volcanic eruption.
The economic aftercast of the policy change was difficult to predict.
They studied the social aftercast of the war.

senses.definition: Clouding that occurs in the eye after cataract surgery, usually behind the artificial lens.
examples: The patient developed an aftercataract a year after surgery.
Laser treatment is often used to clear an aftercataract.
Doctors warned her about the risk of aftercataract.

senses.definition: A later cause or factor that appears after an initial cause and helps produce a result.
examples: Migration acted as an important aftercause of the city’s growth.
The drought became an aftercause of social unrest.
Several aftercauses combined to create the crisis.

senses.definition: A later or additional chance that comes after an earlier opportunity.
examples: He hoped for an afterchance to correct his mistake.
There was no afterchance to retake the exam.
An unexpected afterchance allowed her to apply again.

senses.definition: A color or shade that appears after an original color, especially in photographic or chemical processes.
examples: The image displayed a faint afterchrome as it developed.
Researchers noted an afterchrome in the chemical solution.
The artist experimented with afterchrome effects on the print.

senses.definition: Happening after a church service.
examples: They went to an afterchurch gathering at a friend’s house.
An afterchurch meeting was held in the hall.
She baked cakes for the afterchurch refreshments.

senses.definition: An unexpected and often unpleasant result or consequence that comes after an event.
examples: The afterclap of the decision was a wave of protests.
They feared an afterclap from the media.
The scandal’s afterclap damaged several careers.

senses.definition: Plural of afterclap: several later, usually unpleasant, results or consequences of an event.
examples: The war had many afterclaps that lasted for decades.
They were still dealing with the afterclaps of the scandal.
Economic afterclaps followed the sudden policy change.

senses.definition: A clause that comes after another clause in a sentence, often adding extra information or a condition.
examples: The afterclause explained the conditions of the offer.
In legal writing, an afterclause can limit the meaning of the main clause.
He added an afterclause beginning with "which".

senses.definition: To come or happen later; to follow in time.
examples: Any problems that aftercome will be dealt with calmly.
The true meaning may only aftercome with experience.
Peace did not aftercome until years later.

senses.definition: Someone who comes later, especially a person of a later generation.
examples: He wanted to leave a better world for aftercomers.
The monument was built for the benefit of aftercomers.
Aftercomers may judge these events differently.

senses.definition: Coming later in time; belonging to a later generation or period.
examples: Aftercoming generations may forget these struggles.
The document was written for aftercoming readers.
They planted trees as a gift to aftercoming people.

senses.definition: A device used to cool air or gas after it has been compressed.
examples: The engine’s aftercooler helps reduce the intake air temperature.
A faulty aftercooler can lower engine efficiency.
Technicians cleaned the aftercooler to improve performance.

senses.definition: A cost that appears after the main purchase or project, such as maintenance or follow‑up expenses.
examples: They calculated the aftercost of maintaining the new system.
The cheap machine turned out to have a very high aftercost.
Customers should consider the aftercost before buying.

senses.definition: A later course or path that something or someone follows after an earlier part.
examples: The river’s aftercourse bends sharply to the east.
The aftercourse of his career surprised everyone.
They studied the disease’s aftercourse in patients.

senses.definition: A crop that grows or is planted after the main crop has been harvested.
examples: Farmers planted an aftercrop of vegetables in late summer.
The aftercrop provided extra income for the family.
Clover was used as an aftercrop to improve the soil.

senses.definition: Additional treatment or process that continues after an initial cure or hardening.
examples: The material needs an aftercure to reach full strength.
An aftercure was applied to ensure durability.
The coating undergoes an aftercure at a lower temperature.

senses.definition: A poisonous mixture of gases left in a mine after an explosion or fire, mainly containing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and nitrogen.
examples: Rescue teams could not enter the tunnel because of afterdamp.
The miners were killed not by the blast but by the afterdamp.
Sensors detected high levels of afterdamp in the shaft.

senses.definition: Plural of afterdamp: poisonous gas mixtures left in mines after explosions or fires.
examples: The report described several types of afterdamps found in coal mines.
Equipment is designed to detect afterdamps quickly.
Rescuers must avoid areas filled with afterdamps.

senses.definition: To mark something, especially a document or check, with a date later than the actual date.
examples: He decided to afterdate the contract for accounting reasons.
It is illegal to afterdate official records.
They refused to afterdate the check.

senses.definition: Marked with a date later than the actual date.
examples: The afterdated invoice caused confusion in the accounts.
An afterdated check may not be accepted by the bank.
They signed an afterdated agreement.

senses.definition: The days that come after a particular time or event; later days.
examples: In the afterdays of the war, people tried to rebuild their lives.
Those afterdays were full of uncertainty and hope.
She often spoke about the afterdays of her illness.

senses.definition: Matters or arrangements that happen after an agreement or deal has been made.
examples: The afterdeal required careful negotiation.
Lawyers handled the afterdeal once the contract was signed.
They were disappointed with the afterdeal conditions.

senses.definition: The period or state that is believed to exist after a person dies.
examples: Different religions have their own ideas about afterdeath.
He wrote a book on visions of afterdeath.
Her beliefs about afterdeath gave her comfort.

senses.definition: The deck at the back part of a ship.
examples: Passengers relaxed on the afterdeck in the evening sun.
The crew stored equipment on the afterdeck.
They watched the wake from the ship’s afterdeck.

senses.definition: Plural of afterdeck: decks at the back parts of ships.
examples: Crew members checked the cargo on the afterdecks.
The storm washed water over the afterdecks.
Passengers were asked to stay off the afterdecks in rough seas.
